queue_entry: formatters.ts: split formatters by domain and centralize tool formatter dispatch

notes: formatters.ts is 1180 LOC with 37 exported formatter units spanning table/core output, graph/diff output, analysis reports, and Next.js reports
CodeSift complexity: formatReviewDiff CC29, formatTraceRoute CC15, formatTable CC14; hotspot score 14454 with 2 commits and 33 changed lines in 90 days
CodeSift audit_scan found CQ11 size/complexity findings but no CQ8, CQ13, CQ14, or CQ17 findings; hidden dead-code/clone/cycle tools were unavailable and will be covered by fallbacks

plan.summary: Split formatters.ts into domain modules and use a typed tool-name dispatch map while preserving the formatters.ts facade and all existing exports.

extractions.unit: table, search, outline, repository, context, and general analysis formatters
call graph, route, diff, impact, knowledge map, and community formatters
review, performance, coupling, and architecture formatters
Next.js component, route, metadata, security, API, boundary, data-flow, middleware, framework, and link formatters
tool-name to formatter-function registry and dispatch helper
stable compatibility facade and domain re-exports

plan.dependency_impact: formatters.ts has direct importers in register-tool-groups/deps.ts, retrieval/codebase-retrieval.ts, formatters-shortening.ts, and storage/usage-stats.ts; facade re-exports preserve these paths. Core/analysis/nextjs handlers will use the dispatch helper.
plan.existing_reuse: formatTable remains the shared table primitive; formatters-shortening.ts remains separate and continues importing the facade.
plan.test_strategy: Add pre-refactor characterization coverage for every exported formatter unit and dispatch-map key, then run existing formatter/review tests unchanged.

test_audit_after.command: npm test -- --maxWorkers=2 tests/formatters/formatters-characterization.test.ts tests/formatters/formatter-dispatch.test.ts tests/formatters/format-table.test.ts tests/formatters/nextjs-formatter.test.ts tests/formatters/nextjs-metadata-formatter.test.ts tests/formatters/route-formatter.test.ts tests/formatters/shortening.test.ts tests/tools/review-diff-tools.test.ts tests/retrieval/codebase-retrieval.test.ts tests/tools/register-tools.test.ts
test_audit_after.result: 272 tests passed after the split; formatter facade, dispatch, retrieval, and registration compatibility covered

notes: Independent CQ auditor: facade/export compatibility 37/37, no normalized formatter-body differences, changed dispatch call sites complete, scoped formatter graph acyclic
CQ10 and CQ11 deductions are non-blocking: the generic dispatcher boundary uses data as never, and several moved formatter functions retain legacy complexity/size
Machine audit: CQ8, CQ13, CQ14, and CQ17 pass; CQ11 reports five pre-existing complexity findings in moved formatter bodies

prove.findings_disposition: preserved:6 moved-verbatim hardening observations; no behavior changes; one earlier provider found the same class of pre-existing formatter risks

progress.result: CodeSift indexed; project profile partial; TypeScript and Vitest confirmed from repository files; safety gate installer unavailable
37 exported units mapped; domain split and dispatch map planned; hidden niche tools require degraded fallbacks

progress.command: npm test -- --maxWorkers=2 formatter characterization and existing formatter/review suites
progress.result: 7 files passed, 175 tests passed on PRE_REFACTOR_SHA=4cad382; all 37 exported formatter units exercised before production edits

progress.command: split formatter domains; add formatter-dispatch; route core/analysis/nextjs handlers through dispatch map
